name: Verifiable Agent-Orchestration Swarm
description: Multi-agent task coordination where the coordination is cap-secured, receipted, and verifiable. A factory-born COORDINATOR (dispatch-board) cell holds a conserved budget + a mandate and dispatches sub-tasks to WORKER agent cells via cap-attenuated grants (an over-grant is REFUSED by the verified executor's no-amplification gate); workers coordinate via the async NOTIFY edge (EmitEvent -> the recipient DRAINS in its OWN receipted turn, causality visible and independence proven by two distinct receipt hashes); the shared budget is CONSERVED (spent_a + spent_b <= budget, the affine gate) and a breach is REFUSED BEFORE it runs (fail-closed, no commit); every action leaves a receipt (narration-vs-truth). The dispatch board's installed CellProgram IS the swarm policy, re-checked by the verified executor on every turn (immutable mandate, monotone meters, strict-monotone epoch for no-replay). Rust mirror of metatheory/Dregg2/Apps/AgentOrchestrationBudget.lean (the dispatch-board POLICY, the integrator wedge) + AgentOrchestration.lean (the kernel-executor swarm with attenuated, non-amplifying delegation). Pre-submission assurance via dregg-userspace-verify::analyze (conservation + non-amplification + well-formedness).
